about: Target move-trace for generate-then-read parity (spec-planner.md §13, spec-generation.md 'generate-then-read parity'). Decomposed by hand from a compelling Claude essay about the program's own philosophy; see docs/essay-backwards.md. Each atom is one deposited sentence. `register` is the ground it resolves against: EXT = a node/edge of the subject concept graph; SELF-k = it operates on the proposition already deposited at atom k. `freshSpan` is whether it consumes a fresh external span — the column that shows an essay is ~75% self-operation, which the current resolver (src/longgen/resolve.js) cannot produce.

atoms.gist: a small model is not stupid, it is fluent past its knowledge
hand it a gap and it fills it, that is all it was trained to do
the fill is confident-and-wrong, indistinguishable from confident-and-right
the problem was never competence, it was the open slot
the wager: close the slot without a bigger model
never ask the model to decide what can be decided where it is checked
a separate engine, the planner, makes every structural decision first
it chooses next, resolves to one proposition, hands over one sentence
the model shrinks to the thing it is good at: proposition to sentence
nothing is left open, so nothing to confabulate into
the deeper move: where the planner gets its decisions
it reads the source like the model would, keeps the reading not the prose
every planned claim is one it can point at: a span, an edge
a sentence it cannot ground, it does not plan
when the render drifts off the span, the floor truncates or drops it
grounding becomes a gate every sentence passes, not a hope
the hard case: long generation, each sentence follows the last
the planner switches what it reads from the document to its own output
it reads what it said the way it read the source, plans the continuation
it reads its own sentences with the verdict attached, never the bare claim
a drifted sentence raises strain, a confabulating run turns toward stopping
length is a consequence of how much it can still ground, not a target
one idea at two scales: low says what high admits, high wants what low can ground

summary.finding: 13/23 atoms consume no external span; another 5 only lean on one concept while their content is a relation to a prior atom. The current resolver (resolve.js) launders CON/SIG/INS/REC/DEF/SEG/NUL into assertProposition(ranked[0]) and stops with ground-exhausted when the span pool empties (~3 atoms in). It walks nodes; an essay walks edges.
